description:
Extend the same compiler-macro model to the remaining top-level scope-shaping definition families: `defmodule`, `defprotocol`, and `defimpl`. Their source spellings should expand into canonical compiler-defined fragments, and nested discovery/indexing/publication should proceed from those fragments rather than from special direct raw-source handlers.

This ticket should absorb the pain of reworking nested discovery, publication ordering, export binding, and protocol/impl indexing so that those forms no longer need their own parallel direct-publication semantics.

Signal: nested module/protocol/impl discovery and publication happen from compiler-defined fragments, not bespoke direct source handlers, and import/require/test surfaces still resolve through normal source expansion.
title: Compiler2 scope-shaping definition macros: move defmodule/defprotocol/defimpl onto compiler-defined fragments
